cards.title: Having children makes you smarter? Incredible, but true
cards.body: Mothers and fathers have healthier, younger-looking brains according to recent studies. The mental and emotional gymnastics of parenting appear to boost cognitive function.

cards.title: I kept fearing the worst on our family holiday
cards.body: A parent learns to manage anxiety during family vacation when a real emergency tests their coping abilities. Worry doesn't have to stop you from being present with your children.

cards.title: I wish my son wanted to spend more time with me
cards.body: A parent seeks advice about their adult son's limited contact despite weekly touch-ins. A therapist explores whether unexamined expectations might be creating pressure on the relationship.

cards.title: The Dad Nobody Screens For
cards.body: Postpartum issues aren't limited to mothers—fathers can experience withdrawal and emotional struggles too. Only one parent received screening despite both being affected.

cards.title: This Is, Hands Down, the Most Important Parenting Skill
cards.body: Accepting your child's big feelings, even irrational ones, helps them learn emotional regulation. This simple skill is more powerful than any behavior management technique.

cards.title: Don't Let Parenthood Cost You Your Relationship
cards.body: Two-thirds of couples struggle after a baby arrives, but the other third show us the way. Protecting your partnership strengthens your entire family.

cards.title: Raising Emotionally Healthy Kids in the Digital Age
cards.body: Veteran educators share practical strategies for nurturing children's emotional well-being amid screens and technology. Digital literacy must include emotional intelligence skills.

cards.title: Psychopathy in Children: What Can Be Done?
cards.body: Early diagnosis and intervention are crucial for children showing psychopathic traits or callous-unemotional behavior. Schools play a vital role in identifying and supporting these children.
